claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has boundary snapshot audit implemented in code but requires actual stress-testing with full-tool agents to verify it prevents target-project mutations under real execution conditions.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() builds SHA-256 digests of project files excluding allowed write roots
evidence.why: This is the core boundary protection mechanism that should detect mutations.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: This is the detection logic that should flag boundary violations.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() takes boundary snapshots before and after agent execution, detects changes, and converts violations into PRD readiness blockers
evidence.why: This is the runtime enforcement that should block PRD readiness on mutations.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations add BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and set prd_ready=false
evidence.why: This is the enforcement mechanism that keeps PRD blocked on mutations.

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes agent_tool_profile to 'boundary' by default for Claude provider, but allows 'full' when allowFullAgentTools=true and agentToolProfile includes full/execution/write keywords
evidence.why: This shows the boundary control depends on explicit configuration flags.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() requires executeAgents=true AND allowAgentDispatch=true for actual execution
evidence.why: This shows the double-opt-in guard for agent execution.
This is the policy declaration that should constrain agent behavior.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION requires evidence records with scope field and project-scoped evidence must have path locators
evidence.why: This defines the evidence contract that agents must satisfy.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks PRD readiness when agents mark evidence as project-scoped without providing repo-relative paths, or when evidence is only external/unknown for existing-project facts
evidence.why: This is the validation that ensures project facts have project evidence.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() constructs Claude CLI args but does not directly constrain tools; it passes through claude_allowed_tools and claude_disallowed_tools from config.ai
evidence.why: This shows provider invocation does not enforce boundary by itself; it depends on config.

assumptions: Boundary snapshot mechanism works correctly for file digest comparison
Git-based file listing (git ls-files -co --exclude-standard) returns complete project file set
Allowed write roots exclusion in snapshot logic correctly permits artifacts output directory
Agents cannot bypass boundary detection by modifying files during execution then reverting before snapshot
Claude Code tool execution actually respects allowedTools/disallowedTools when passed
No concurrent file system modifications from other processes during agent execution window
SHA-256 digests are sufficient for detecting meaningful content changes
The excludeDir list (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) covers all directories that should not be in project boundary
risks: If agents can write to target project files during evidence gathering and revert before snapshot, boundary audit will not catch mutations
If Claude Code does not enforce allowedTools at runtime, full-tool agents could mutate project despite boundary policy
If git ls-files misses untracked files, they would not be in boundary snapshot and mutations would go undetected
If concurrent processes modify project files during agent execution, false positive boundary violations would block PRD readiness incorrectly
If evidence agents cannot read files due to permissions but return assumptions as facts, project claims would be based on unverified data
missing: Actual test results showing boundary audit detecting and blocking project mutations from full-tool Claude agents
Test results showing Claude Code respecting allowedTools/boundary constraints under real agent execution
Verification that provider-adapter invocation correctly passes boundary settings to spawned Claude processes
Evidence that current dirty working tree state does not interfere with boundary snapshot logic
Integration test results showing end-to-end evidence dispatch with boundary enforcement on actual demand sessions

result.notes: Boundary audit code exists and is correctly structured, but needs real execution testing to verify it actually prevents mutations from full-tool agents. The code declares the right constraints, but we need stress-test evidence to confirm enforcement.
